blocks.content: On the way toward Callaway Park they passed Mr. Whitaker, crouched over a sprinkler valve in his front yard with the expression of a man losing to a machine.
The old man looked up. Took in the four of them.
Mr. Whitaker was quiet for a beat.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Whitaker
blocks.content: I climbed that tower in 1962. It was condemned then too.
He looked back at his sprinkler valve.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Whitaker
blocks.content: Don't tell anybody I told you that.
He did something to the valve that seemed satisfactory, stood up carefully, and looked at his lawn — the greenest on the street — and then at the sky.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Whitaker
blocks.content: It's dry. Been praying for rain all week.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Whitaker
blocks.content: Always does. Just never when you're ready for it.
He went up to the porch and came back with four Coca-Colas in glass bottles, cold from the back of the cooler, sweating the second they hit the afternoon air.
They said thank you. He waved them off and went back to his sprinkler like it was nothing.
